attributes.title: UnitedHealth, Humana seen as managed care beneficiaries in second Trump term
attributes.content:
• UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH), Humana (NYSE:HUM), and CVS Health (NYSE:CVS), which own Aetna, are seen as managed care providers set to benefit the most should former President Donald Trump win a second term in November.
  • RBC Capital Markets on Friday released a note stating that following Thursday night's debate between Trump and President Joe Biden, they see a more favorable regulatory environment under a Trump presidency.
  • "A second Trump term would ease regulatory and reimbursement headwinds weighing on the managed care stocks, particularly the Medicare Advantage leaders UNH, HUM, and CVS," analyst Ben Hendrix wrote.
  • "Among the MCOs, we see the most immediate upside for UNH as we believe a Trump administration would also ease DOJ scrutiny around Optum and vertical integration between health plans and Optum Health's primary are platform," he added.

More on UnitedHealth, Humana

  • UnitedHealth Group: Uncertainties Through 2025 Reiterate Buy Opportunity
  • I'm Sticking To My Call: UnitedHealth Is Among The Best Dividend Growers
  • Humana: Medicare Advantage Will Never Be As Profitable As It Was
  • UnitedHealth PBM OptumRx in $20M federal settlement over opioid prescriptions
  • UnitedHealth and Cigna PBMs face Arkansas lawsuit over role in opioid crisis

attributes.title: Align Technology added as a new short idea at Hedgeye
attributes.content:
Align Technology (NASDAQ:ALGN) was added as a new short idea at Hedgeye with the potential for 20% downside from current levels.

"Our data suggests continued softness in ALGN's North American GP utilization heading into 2Q24 and slight miss in 2Q24 revenue, a decent catalyst for the short, especially after the company recently raised guidance for 2024," Hedgeye analyst Tom Tobin wrote in a note after the close on Thursday.

"ALGN shares correlate strongest with EPS estimates with a seemingly hard floor of 20X forward P/E," Tobin added. "We expect a softer outlook for 2H24, rising macro concerns, and lower estimates for 2025 can break the 20X floor and push ALGN below $200, or 20% lower from here.

The potential issues for Align (ALGN) include slowing real income growth, rising treatment financing costs and accelerating dental labor costs, Tobin wrote.

Align (ALGN) short interest is 2.2%.

Shares of Align (ALGN) ticked down by 0.3% on Friday.

attributes.title: Prudential's PGIM launches two active muni bond ETFs
attributes.content:
PGIM, the $1.34T investment management business of Prudential Financial (NYSE:PRU), has rolled out two exchange-traded funds tied to municipal bonds, it said on Friday.

The new funds — PGIM Ultra Short Municipal Bond ETF (PUSH) and PGIM Municipal Income Opportunities ETF (PMIO) — will trade on NYSE Arca.

Both actively-managed ETFs invest at least 80% of their respective portfolios in municipal obligations whose income is exempt from federal income taxes.

PUSH, in particular, will mainly invest in investment-grade muni bonds and up to 10% in high-yield muni debt obligations. The fund seeks to maintain a weighted average portfolio duration of two years or less.

PMIO, by comparison, will invest at least 70% of its portfolio in investment-grade muni debt obligations and up to 30% in high yield muni debt obligations. It seeks to maintain a weighted average portfolio duration of two to eight years.

“In addition to their diversification benefits, muni bond ETFs offer an attractive opportunity for investors, particularly high-net-worth investors, who may be looking to maximize tax efficiency within their portfolios," said Stuart Parker, president and CEO of PGIM Investments.

More on Prudential Financial

  • Prudential Financial: This Dividend Growth Stock Remains A Buy
  • Prudential Financial, Inc. (PRU) Q1 2024 Earnings Call Transcript

attributes.title: VSE Corp. started at Buy at Stifel as aviation aftermarket growth story
attributes.content:
VSE Corp. (NASDAQ:VSEC) +2% in Friday's trading as Stifel initiates coverage with a Buy rating and a $101 price target, seeing the stock as an aviation aftermarket growth story that is consolidating share in distribution and repairs at a time when the macro environment is very supportive.

VSE (VSEC) is "in the middle innings of a transformation from a logistics, supply chain and distribution company with a multi-sector focus to an aviation aftermarket pure play with defensible barriers in both the commercial and B&GA end markets," Stifel's Bert Subin writes.

The analyst sees upside stemming from the anticipated separation of the Fleet segment and forecast continued double-digit growth in Aviation for the next several quarters on the back of industry growth, share gains and M&A.

VSE (VSEC) shares "are on a nice run over the last year, but we think there's additional upside from here as margins rise and the focus shifts fully to aerospace markets," Subin says.

More on VSE Corp.

  • Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating on VSE Corp.
  • Historical earnings data for VSE Corp.
  • VSE Corp.: Strong Growth Outlook But Share Price Lacks Margin Of Safety

attributes.title: Everbridge ticks higher amid Nasdaq delisting notice for Thoma Bravo sale
attributes.content:
Everbridge (NASDAQ:EVBG) edged higher by 0.8% after a Nasdaq delisting notice for its planned sale to Thomas Bravo.

The deal is tentatively scheduled to close prior to the market open on Tuesday, according to the Nasdaq notice on Friday. In anticipation of the closing, the stock will be halted immediately following the after-hours session at 8 p.m. on Monday.

The notice comes after Everbridge (EVBG) on Tuesday said it expected its planned sale to Thoma Bravo will be completed in Q3, after earlier forecasting a Q2 close. The parties continue to pursue the one remaining outstanding regulatory approval in Sweden required to satisfy the closing conditions set forth in the merger agreement, according to an 8-K filing at the time.

Thoma Bravo originally agreed to buy enterprise software company Everbridge (EVBG) in February, though the deal price was increased to $35 a share in March.

attributes.title: International Paper upgraded at Seaport after Suzano ends bid
attributes.content:
International Paper (NYSE:IP) is upgraded Friday to Buy from Neutral with a $52 price target at Seaport Global, following a nearly 9% decline in the two days after Suzano dropped its takeover pursuit, which Seaport says brings the stock back to a reasonable entry point.

Profitability in containerboard is improving from multiyear lows, and fundamentals should tighten, Seaport analyst Mark Weintraub says, foreseeing 200-500 bps of margin catch-up and/or improvement potential in International Paper's (IP) North American Industrial Packaging business, as well as improved near-term profitability in Global Cellulose Fibers with divestiture of the business in the year ahead a distinct possibility.

New International Paper (IP) CEO Andy Silvernail enjoyed great success at Idex and brings not only fresh perspective to the company but likely new investors as well, Weintraub says.

Meanwhile, Seaport updates its 2024 estimates and 2025 rollout numbers for International Paper (IP), projecting solid Y/Y improvement, much of it driven by higher pricing already in process.

More on International Paper

  • International Paper: Catalysts On The Horizon But No Rush To Jump In

attributes.title: The AI-themed stocks have contributed to half of the returns of the MSCI ACWI - Citi
attributes.content:
Year-to-date, the artificial intelligence theme has driven half of the MSCI All County World Index’s (NASDAQ:ACWI) 11% return, with stocks that have high exposure to the theme benefitting from expanding multiples and upwards earnings revisions, according to a Citi Research Thematic Equity Strategy report.

Also, the theme has driven almost 60% of global equity market returns this year.

Companies with high exposure to AI drove almost 50% of the MSCI ACWI (ACWI) year-to-date return, although they only had a 14% weight in the index, analysts said.

Stocks with medium AI exposure saw another 13% of the index market cap within the MSCI ACWI (ACWI).

In addition, stocks with high AI exposure are up 36%, and stocks with medium exposure are up 9% year-to-date. The rest of the index -- non-AI stocks -- have lagged, gaining only 6% so far this year.

Nvidia (NVDA) leads the list of the top 10 AI-themed contributors to the MSCI ACWI (ACWI) returns. The stock has accounted for almost 3% of the index’s 11% year-to-date return alone.

The remaining “magnificent seven” stocks in the list -- including Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOGL), Meta Platforms (META), Amazon (AMZN), and Apple (AAPL) -- account for almost another 3% combined.

Other AI contributors to MSCI ACWI (ACWI) returns year-to-date include Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) with 0.37%, Broadcom (AVGO) with 0.32%, ASML Holding (ASML) with 0.16%, and Tencent Holding (OTCPK:TCEHY) with 0.11% contribution.

In addition, of the 36% return for high AI stocks, 22% is driven by P/E re-rating and 14% is driven by upward EPS revisions, analysts noted.

“Given the aggressive move in this group of stocks, one may actually expect more of the year-to-date return to come from valuations which typically move faster than earnings estimates,” wrote Analyst Drew Pettit.

“For medium AI exposure and non-AI groups, valuation expansion has been a tailwind, but full-year earnings estimate cuts have been a modest offset," he said. "Both groups are still up year-to-date and positive contributors to index advances but have not had the same dual tailwind of the more AI exposed names.”

More on iShares MSCI ACWI ETF:

  • ACWI: Go With U.S. Funds Instead
  • The best of breed global stocks for 3Q2024 - BofA
  • Stocks with the largest market cap in the U.S. from the 1950s to today - MSIM
  • Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating on iShares MSCI ACWI ETF
  • Dividend scorecard for iShares MSCI ACWI ETF

attributes.title: Apple regains shine; receives average price target hike of 19% since WWDC
attributes.content:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) regained its shine among investment firms after unveiling Apple Intelligence at the WWDC on June 10.

The company now appears poised to dominate the "consumer AI experience" and recently received a slate of price target hikes as analysts now expect the stock to climb considerably in the coming quarters.

Oppenheimer increased its price target by 25% to $250 from $200 and reiterated its Outperform rating on the Cupertino darling.

"Apple is setting a new standard for hardware and software experience for a technology implementation that can appear confusing, intimidating, and jarring for an average consumer," writes Oppenheimer analysts Martin Yang and Andrew Northcutt, in a Friday note. "We believe Apple's solution to bring AI to consumers, alongside its unique ecosystem advantage, would result in faster than expected acceleration of revenue and EPS growth."

The integration of AI into iOS18 could accelerate a replacement cycle across Apple's ever-popular hardware line of iPhones, iPads and Macs. Oppenheimer also stresses the strength of Apple's moat, which includes a hardware user base of 2.2B installed devices, vertical integration from silicon to software and a loyal fan base that remains unmatched by competitors.

The latest batch of price targets range from $164 to $275 as investment firms have increased their targets on the stock by an average of 19% since June 10:

  • Wedbush Securities set the highest bar with a price target of $275 on June 11, just one day after the WWDC kickoff.
  • Melius Research hiked its target to $260 from $227 on June 24.
  • Rosenblatt increased its price target to $260 from $196 on June 26.
  • Oppenheimer upped its target to $250 from $200 on June 28.
  • Evercore ISI bumped its target to $250 from $220 on June 25.
  • J.P. Morgan boosted its target to $245 from $225 on June 18.
  • Bernstein increased it price target to $240 from $195 on June 21.
  • Bank of America assigned a price target at $230 on June 11.
  • D.A. Davidson set a target of $230 on June 11.
  • PhillipCapital raised its target to $220 from $194 on June 18.
  • Barclays had a price target of $164 as of June 11.

Apple's view among investors changed rapidly after the tech titan flexed its muscles on June 10. Before that, its stock ranked as the worst performer year to date, other than Tesla (TSLA), out of the Magnificent Seven. Apple shares are up 11% since June 10.

attributes.title: Bitcoin, blockchain, India among top-performing themes of H1 2024: Ned Davis Research
attributes.content:
Exchange-traded funds focused on bitcoin (BTC-USD), blockchain technology and the Indian economy represented the top-performing themes during H1 2024, Ned Davis Research said.

The Grayscale Bitcoin Trust ETF (GBTC) topped NDR’s list released Thursday as part of its mid-year outlook. Grayscale’s John Hoffman at the Seeking Alpha Summit last week predicted that crypto is "going to be in every portfolio” over the next 20 years.

So far in 2024, the MSCI World Index (URTH) has gained 11%. The S&P 500 (SP500)(SPY)(VOO) has advanced ~15%, powered largely by mega-cap tech stocks including Nvidia (NVDA) and Meta (META).

Here’s NDR’s list of themes and ETFs with YTD performances through early Friday. *ETF's weight in the 'Magnificent 7' are in parenthesis:

  • Theme: Bitcoin - Grayscale Bitcoin Trust ETF (GBTC) - YTD gain: 54.5%
  • Theme: Blockchain - Amplify Transformational Data Sharing ETF (BLOK)*(1.4) - YTD gain: 20.3%
  • Theme: India - Columbia India Consumer (INCO) - YTD gain: 19.2%
  • Theme: 5G & IoT - Defiance 5G Next Gen Connectivity ETF (FIVG) *(12.9) - YTD gain: 16.5%
  • Theme: Video Games - VanEck Video Gaming and eSports ETF (ESPO) - YTD gain: 18.3%
  • Theme: Climate Change - Xtrackers MSCI USA Climate Action Equity ETF (USCA)*(32.0) - YTD gain: 16.3%
  • SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY)*(32.5) - YTD gain: 15.3%
  • Theme: AI & Automation - Global X Artificial Intelligence & Technology ETF (AIQ)*(24.1) - YTD gain: 14.7%
  • Theme: ESG - iShares ESG Aware MSCI USA ETF (ESGU)*(31.0) - YTD gain: 14.6%
  • Theme: Metaverse - Roundhill Ball Metaverse ETF (METV)*(33.7) - YTD gain: 14.5%
  • Theme: Environmental Services - VanEck Environmental Services ETF (EVX) - YTD gain: 11.9%
  • Theme: MLPs - Alerian MLP ETF (AMLP) - YTD gain: 12%
  • Theme: Millennials Global X Millennials Consumer ETF (MILN)*(12.8) - YTD gain: 9.1%
  • Theme: Gold/Silver Miners - VanEck Gold Miners ETF (GDX) - YTD gain: 10.7%

attributes.title: Live Nation gains as antitrust trial may not be until 2026, potential for Trump win
attributes.content:
Live Nation (NYSE:LYV) rose 4% amid a report that the U.S. Justice Department's antitrust lawsuit against the concert promoter won't go to trial until 20026. The shares may also be helped by higher odds of a Donald Trump win after the Presidential debate Thursday night.

U.S. District Judge Arun Subramanian called the timeline "appropriate," according to a Reuters report on Thursday, recounting the first hearing held on the trial in New York. Live Nation (LYV) is pushing for the case to be transferred to Washington, D.C. Subramanian met for about an hour on Thursday with lawyers for both sides.

Late last month, the DOJ and more than two dozen states filed an antitrust suit against Ticketmaster parent Live Nation (LYV), looking to break up the company.

Shares of Live Nation (LYV) may also be moving up after the Presidential debate on Thursday night as investors see a potential Trump presidency as more positive for antitrust matters as the Biden administration has been particularly strict.

More on Live Nation Entertainment

  • Live Nation Entertainment: Scale Advantage In A Growing Industry
  • Live Nation Entertainment, Inc. (LYV) Q1 2024 Earnings Call Transcript

attributes.title: Lake Resources cuts staff, plans asset sales as lithium prices stay weak
attributes.content:
Lake Resources (OTCQB:LLKKF) said it will lay off most of its employees and sell assets, as low lithium prices are hurting attempts to find customers and investors for its flagship Kachi project in Argentina, Reuters reported late Thursday.

The Kachi project in the mountainous Catamarca province attracted preliminary interest from Ford and others, but operating at the remote site caused extensive cost overruns that became untenable once lithium prices began to slide.

Prices for lithium carbonate are trading below $15K/metric ton, according to Fastmarkets, and Lake Resources (OTCQB:LLKKF) CEO David Dickson said he believes many in the lithium market need to sign supply contracts with price floors of at least $15K-$20K/ton in order to make projects economical.

Lake Resources (OTCQB:LLKKF) is now looking to sell four lithium-rich acreage positions it controls elsewhere in Argentina and likely will move out of its Houston offices to a smaller location, Dickson said.

More on Lake Resources

attributes.title: CB, TRV stocks fall after downgrade by William Blair
attributes.content:
Chubb (NYSE:CB) fell 1.8% after William Blair downgraded the insurer to Underperform from Market Perform due to a worsening casualty environment.

"The casualty side of the insurance industry is being hit by rising levels of claims and losses," William Blair said.

Earnings growth is poised to slide due to a more challenging claims environment and decelerating investment income growth, the analysts added.

The firm reduced their 2025 operating EPS estimate for Chubb (CB) to $20.69 from $23.50 and initiated its 2026 estimate at $20.13.

"Chubb’s is one of the strongest competitors in the market. However, significant positioning in casualty lines has the potential to be a drag on earnings going forward," the analysts said.

William Blair also downgraded Travelers (NYSE:TRV) to Market Perform from Outperform.

"As with Chubb and the overall market, Travelers is experiencing rising severity levels across key lines of business," the note stated.

For TRV, the analysts reduced the 2025 operating EPS estimate to $17.70 from $19.10 and initiated the 2026 estimate at $15.94.

"The potential for slowing earnings growth will likely hinder the share performance of Chubb and Travelers over the next few years," the analysts said in a note.

The analysts expect that the recent trend of deepening severity across key lines at these insurers will lead to rising loss ratios and declining earnings growth.

TRV was down 1%.

SA quant rates CB at Strong Buy, while analysts rate it at Buy.

For TRV, both SA quant and analysts' rating stand at Buy.

More on Chubb, The Travelers

attributes.title: Jefferies Q2 results point to improving IB outlook for big banks, Mike Mayo says
attributes.content:
Jefferies' (NYSE:JEF) stronger-than-expected fiscal Q2 results indicate an improving outlook for investment banking among big lenders, Wells Fargo (WFC) analyst Mike Mayo wrote in a Friday note.

Shares of New York-based Jefferies (JEF) extended gains from Thursday's spike, advancing 3% in late morning trading, after delivering fiscal Q2 earnings that surpassed the average analyst estimate as positive momentum continued to build across its investment banking and capital markets businesses.

The company recorded nearly 60% Y/Y growth in investment banking net revenues, easily outpacing what the big banks have guided for.

"Jefferies 50% growth appears outsized, but could point to better-than-guided IB results for large banks," Mayo wrote.

Trading growth at Jefferies was also above implied guidance for larger banks, he noted. JEF's capital markets net revenues gained 24% from a year before.

Too-big-to-fail banks include: Citigroup (C), Bank of America (BAC), Goldman Sachs (GS), JPMorgan Chase (JPM) and Morgan Stanley (MS). The megabanks will be posting their Q2 earnings starting mid-July.

More on Jefferies Financial Group

  • Jefferies: Full Valuation For A Cyclical Business
  • Jefferies: Attractive Global Expansion At A Fair Price
  • Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating on Jefferies Financial Group

attributes.title: Polestar Automotive falls after publishing full-year 2023 results
attributes.content:
Polestar Automotive Holding UK (NASDAQ:PSNY) fell after the electric vehicle maker reported its preliminary unaudited financial and operational results for 2023.

Polestar (PSNY) also disclosed that previously announced errors identified in the audited 2021 and 2022 accounts have now been corrected and have an impact on net loss of less than 5% in each respective year. The error had a positive effect for one year and negative for the other.

For 2023, Polestar (PSNY) reported revenue of $2.38 billion vs. a restated 2022 tally of $2.45 billion. For 2023, the company reported an adjusted operating loss of $1.46 billion, which was the higher than the restated adjusted operating loss of $917 million for 2022.

In addition, Polestar (PSNY) updated that customer deliveries of Polestar 3 have now started and will ramp up over the summer. It was noted that the first release of customers test-drive slots in Europe have been filled and production in South Carolina remains on track to start during the end of the summer.

Polestar (PSNY) expects to publish its preliminary unaudited results for the first quarter and global deliveries for the second quarter on July 2 before the market opens.

Shares of Polestar (PSNY) were down 6.55% in Friday morning action to $0.77. The restaurant EV stock is down 85% from its 52-week high of $4.96.

attributes.title: Berkshire Hathaway's chief Warren Buffett to donate shares to charity
attributes.content:
Berkshire Hathaway's (NYSE:BRK.A) (NYSE:BRK.B) chief Warren Buffett said on Friday he will convert some of his Class A shares into Class B shares to make another round of donations.

The billionaire investor will convert 8.67K A shares into 13.01M B shares as he looks to further donate 13M shares of the firm's “B” stock to five foundations, namely Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation Trust, Susan Thompson Buffett Foundation, the Sherwood Foundation, Howard G. Buffett Foundation and NoVo Foundation.

However, his donation to the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation will end after his death, Buffett said in an interview with the Wall Street Journal. He also added that after his passing, nearly all of his remaining wealth will go to a new charitable trust overseen by his daughter and two sons.

Susie Buffett chairs the Sherwood Foundation and the Susan Thompson Buffett Foundation, while Howie Buffett heads the Howard G. Buffett Foundation. Peter Buffett and his wife, Jennifer Buffett, lead the NoVo Foundation.

Buffett’s ownership of Berkshire now consists of ~208K A shares and 2.6K B shares.

attributes.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow move higher as markets receive key inflation data
attributes.content:
Major market averages moved up into the green on Friday, a day after the first U.S. presidential debate of the year took place, and as investors received further key inflation data.

Early on and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.6%, the S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.6%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.6%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) dipped 1 basis point to 4.28%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 3 basis points to 4.68%. See how other yields trade across the entire yield curve here.

Wall Street's major market averages concluded in a more muted fashion on Thursday as traders parsed through a slew of economic reports.

Politics was in the spotlight as incumbent President Joe Biden and former leader Donald Trump sparred over key topics at the first televised presidential debate late on Thursday.

"Polls immediately after the debate declared Trump the winner. While incumbents often 'lose' debates, Biden reportedly lost by a large margin. This invites increased investor scrutiny of Trump’s policies, especially around trade," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Headline and core PCE inflation both ease in May. The May PCE Price Index came in at 0.0% M/M, which was in-line with the consensus. Core PCE arrived at +0.1% which also was as forecasted.

On the economic calendar, the June Chicago PMI also hit. The Chicago PMI rose to 47.4, which was more than what was expected.

Moreover, the U.S. consumers dial back their year-ahead inflation expectations in the June consumer sentiment report.

More on markets

  • Dividend Roundup: Kroger, AT&T, Pfizer, JPMorgan, and more
  • Russell Investments believes in a soft landing but warns a recession is still on the table
  • The Greenback Consolidates While Sustaining A Break Against The Yen
  • Fed Update: The Fed Has A Mortgage-Backed Securities Problem
  • USD Pushes Above JPY160

attributes.title: Nutrien CEO Seitz sees fertilizer market finally stabilizing
attributes.content:
Nutrien (NYSE:NTR), the world's top producer of crop nutrients, believes fertilizer demand is just now starting to stabilize after the shocks of recent years that caused wild profit swings, CEO Ken Seitz told Bloomberg in an interview Friday.

"We are looking at demand that is after a very volatile period, with some demand destruction, that is only today returning to sort of trend levels," Seitz said, noting that current fertilizer prices are below the 10-year average.

Nutrien (NTR) has found a particularly sore spot in Brazil, where surging interest rates have made a glut even more difficult to manage; Seitz said farmers are now turning to just-in-time purchases, adding that a pesticide glut will take the rest of this year and possibly into 2025 to be resolved.

In Argentina, where Nutrien (NTR) is seeking to sell its agriculture retail operations and considering options for its 50% interest in the Profertil urea and ammonia venture, the challenge of repatriating dollars is a key problem because of Argentina’s currency controls, the CEO told Bloomberg.

"We don't see any indication that anything is going to change" in Argentina under the new administration of President Javier Milei, Seitz said.

More on Nutrien

  • So Cheap! Why Nutrien Is One Of My Favorite Plays
  • Nutrien: 4% Yielder Nearing Trough Valuation With High Profits
  • Nutrien: A Low Risk Total Return Pick Benefiting From Inflation

attributes.title: Apellis falls as EU experts decline to back eye disorder therapy
attributes.content:
• Apellis Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:APLS) traded lower on Friday after an expert panel of the EU drug regulator, the European Medicines Agency (EMA), declined to back its eye disorder therapy pegcetacoplan.
  • The so-called negative opinion, issued by the EMA's Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP), relates to the company's request to seek marketing authorization for intravitreal pegcetacoplan in geographic atrophy (GA), a leading cause of blindness.
  • The Waltham, Massachusetts-based biotech said it would file for a reevaluation of the CHMP's decision, and a final opinion is expected in Q4 2024.
  • "We remain steadfast in our commitment to GA patients in Europe who have no treatment for this devastating disease, which leads to irreversible vision loss," Apellis' (APLS) chief development officer Jeffrey Eisele said.
  • The drug is already available in the U.S. as Syfovre for GA secondary to age-related macular degeneration following its FDA approval in 2023.

More on Apellis Pharmaceuticals

attributes.title: Russell Investments believes in a soft landing but warns a recession is still on the table
attributes.content:
As the first half of 2024 wraps up, the financial community now shifts its attention towards Q3 and where markets are potentially headed. Russell Investments argued that there is still no clear answer to this year's question of 'is the economy headed towards a no, soft or hard landing?'

“Our base case scenario is for the U.S. economy to achieve a soft landing, but we still see a 35% probability of a recession over the next 12 months,” Russell Investments said in a recent investor note.

Hard Landing

“The case for a hard landing (recession) relies on historical precedent.” The investment institution indicated that the Federal Reserve has embarked on one of its most aggressive tightening campaigns since Fed chairman Paul Volcker. History suggests that the U.S. economy has never previously avoided a recession after such a sustained period of restrictive monetary policy.

Soft Landing

“The potential for a soft landing or a period of below-trend growth is backed by the slowdown in forward-looking labor market indicators.” Some of these indicators include hiring rates, the cooling of wage growth, and signs of distress from lower income consumers, which is seen as risks on rising credit card debts and delayed auto payments.

No Landing

“No landing seems the least likely outcome.” According to Russell Investments, there is enough evidence that the economy is slowing and that inflation pressures are easing.

For investors looking to further track the outlook on the economy, they may want to analyze the broader market averages and U.S. Treasury yields as sentiment gauges. Listed below are a handful of index tracking and Treasury focused ETFs market participants can monitor.

Dow, S&P, and Nasdaq ETFs: (NYSEARCA:DIA), (DDM), (UDOW), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (NASDAQ:QQQ), (QLD), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

Treasury ETFs: (NASDAQ:TLT), (TLH), (IEF), (IEI), (SHY), (SGOV), (SCHO), and (BIL).

attributes.title: U.S. consumers dial back year-ahead inflation expectations in June
attributes.content:
U.S. consumers lowered their inflation expectations for the year ahead, but maintained their inflation expectations at the five-year horizon, The University of Michigan said Friday in its Surveys of Consumers report.

Year-ahead implied inflation: +3.0% vs. +3.3% initial print and +3.3% in May.

Five-year implied inflation: +3.0% vs. +3.1% initial reading and +3.0% prior.

"These expectations remain somewhat elevated relative to the 2.2-2.6% range seen in the two years pre-pandemic," said Surveys of Consumers Director Joanne Hsu.

Consumer sentiment, meantime, slipped to 68.2 from 69.1 in May. The final June print, though, was stronger than the mid-month mark of 65.6.

Expectations Index: 69.6 vs. 67.6 initial estimate and 68.8 in prior month.

Current Conditions: 65.9 vs. 62.5 initial reading and 69.6 in May.

More on the U.S. Economy

  • Mixed Economic Data Raises Questions In U.S. And Around The World
  • Monetary Conditions Are Returning To Normal
  • Headline, core PCE inflation both ease in May, as expected

attributes.title: Repsol in talks to merge U.K. North Sea operations with NEO Energy - Reuters
attributes.content:
Repsol (OTCQX:REPYY) (OTCQX:REPYF) is in talks to merge its U.K. North Sea oil and gas business with private equity-backed NEO Energy, with a deal potentially to be announced within weeks, Reuters reported Friday.

A merger would create a company with production of more than 110K boe/day, making it one of the largest producers in the aging basin.

Repsol Resources UK (OTCQX:REPYY) (OTCQX:REPYF) produced nearly 39K boe/day at the end of 2022, and NEO Energy has interests in 25 fields in the North Sea, where it produces ~80K boe/day.

The company also had a tax loss position of $3.7B, according to its latest filing, and the Labour Party, which commands a large lead in polls ahead of Britain's elections next week, has vowed to increase taxes on the sector, which are currently at a rate of 75%, one of the highest in the world.

attributes.title: Intuitive Machines in spotlight as B Riley initiates coverage with Buy rating, $8 PT
attributes.content:
Intuitive Machines (NASDAQ:LUNR) were in focus on Friday as B Riley Securities initiated coverage on the space exploration company with a Buy rating and a 12-month price target of $8.

"Intuitive has superior capabilities and trade secrets relative to other competitors also vying for a piece of NASA's multi-billion-dollar programs like Commercial Lunar Payload Services," noted analyst Mike Crawford from B Riley securities.

The brokerage added that the company had been the first U.S. corporate to land its spacecraft on the surface of the Moon and the first to soft land on the lunar South Pole. When compared against NASA's Apollo program in 1972 which cost over $25 billion, Intuitive's expenditure for its moon landing mission, Odysseus' budget is two to three orders smaller in magnitude, it said.

Intuitive also boasts of an accomplished team including former NASA Johnson Space Center (JSC) deputy director Steve Altemus, along with experts Dr. Tim Crain and Dr. Kam Ghaffarian.

"Assured lunar access has strong bipartisan support in the U.S. at a time when America and its allies have fallen behind China in the race to space," said the brokerage. "China's continuing success has spurred the development of multiple decadal government programs aimed at bolstering U.S. lunar presence," it added.

Intuitive's current focus on lunar space missions by NASA and its support towards the commercialization of the Moon and beyond, its capabilities in providing lunar landing access, lunar infrastructure, and lunar/cislunar communications, anchored by multi-billion dollar contracts, ally with U.S. interests in space.

In April, LUNR had one among 3 companies selected in a $4.6 billion NASA contract where it will design buggies that will eventually be used by astronauts to travel around on the lunar surface.

"We believe Intuitive is leading the U.S.-allied charge to provide low-cost access to the Moon, with a compelling delivery capability that will drive long-term profits for the company as communications and deployment services kick in on top of an access cadence that we see as only increasing," the brokerage concluded.

Intuitive Machines (LUNR) stock rose +5.65% to $3.74 on Friday, which mark it above its 52-week low of $2.09. The stock has gained 38.55% YTD.

attributes.title: Hyatt acquires me and all hotels brand
attributes.content:
• Hyatt Hotels Corporation (NYSE:H) affiliate has acquired the me and all hotels brand from Lindner Hotels AG, expanding the company’s footprint in Germany with the addition of seven boutique hotels.
  • Terms of the transaction were not disclosed.
  • The acquisition builds on the collaboration between Hyatt and Lindner, and with the subsequent integration, me and all hotels will become a standalone brand within the company’s lifestyle portfolio. The unit is currently a nestled brand within Hyatt’s JdV by Hyatt
  • Me and all hotels are designed for city and business travelers with a blend of luxury and coziness with cutting-edge technology. Currently located in German cities including Düsseldorf, Mainz, Hanover, Kiel and Ulm, me and all hotels is expanding to Berlin, Hamburg, Leipzig, and Stuttgart.
  • Including the addition of me and all hotels into Hyatt’s (H) pipeline, the company has more than 1,300 hotels and all-inclusive properties in 78 countries across six continents.
  • Hyatt (H) shares are 0.5% higher at Friday’s open.
  • Press Release
